Fear and Greed is at
18
(Extreme
Fear)
and BTC slipped to
$61K
this morning. The primary catalyst for yesterday's drop was a 10%
crash in global AI stocks originating in Seoul that swept through
semiconductors and into crypto. Bitcoin moved in tandem with tech
as investors rotated out of high-valuation sectors, with AI stock
weakness turning the day broadly risk-off.

BTC is now testing the $60K psychological floor for the second
time this month. Five converging pressures are in play
simultaneously. Patience is the word analysts keep using.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Trump Just Blocked a CBDC Ban From Becoming Law

President Trump canceled the signing of a major housing bill
on June 24, putting a provision barring the Federal Reserve from
issuing a CBDC until 2030 on hold. The bill had passed the Senate
85-5 and the House 358-32 just days earlier.

Trump linked the cancellation to unrelated voting legislation.

What exception did the housing bill's CBDC ban carve out for
stablecoins?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/Bb8cDArObr5aQ1cv6aHTng~~/AAQRxRA~/bOPUgnJ6ST7QlPZRqaruXU3kNrPvHmiANONq2Dv8ZSX2TNKrS5XeHTbdaQ60o8hD7KGc5GIZnV_qQrNmZqrGf1h7sPqtlN7aPpZVYNZ_2Ab0ZdKBkJ2NlOJ1-3mwzEuHhvua4TRmVt7jEU9HezdRcDPlCLTKr4w-CgQCDGY3gyU~ )
   Bitcoin Is Testing the Four-Year Cycle Again

Bitcoin fell below $60K on June 24 for the second time this
month. ETF issuer 21S

75055.subject: рҹҢ¶пёҸ OpenAI makes... JalapeГұo
75055.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://utm.genai.works/r/he2t9cz)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** OpenAI built its own AI chip and it shows us they need to hire someone to name their products. It runs OpenAI's models for about half the cost, and OpenAI used its own AI to design it, the snake eating its own tail in silicon. Anthropic benched Dario Amodei for Trump . Google's flagship slipped to July, and ChatGPT got tuned to be more fun to talk to.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **OpenAI:** Why did OpenAI build its own chip, and what does it do to Nvidia?

* **Anthropic:** Why did Anthropic bench its own CEO?

* **Google:** Why did Google's flagship slip to July?

* **ChatGPT:** What does OpenAI gain by making ChatGPT more fun?


----------

----------
## [OpenAI develops JalapeГұo](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/24/openai-unveils-its-first-custom-chip-built-by-broadcom/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1ddbbd1a-9d0c-413f-831e-8b236b172b0f/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_25.png?t=1782380702)
Caption: 

OpenAI built its own chip, called jalapeГұo, in collaboration with Broadcom.В 

The new processor has one job, running OpenAI's models cheaply.В 

Broadcom's chief says it does that for roughly half the cost of the Nvidia GPUs everyone rents, which for a company bleeding cash on compute is close to oxygen.

OpenAI used its own models to help design the chip, so the AI built the hardware that runs the AI, and it did it in nine months. It's also a shot at Nvidia, whose GPUs are both OpenAI's biggest cost and its biggest chokehold.В 

Google and Amazon already make their own, so OpenAI joining means the companies that rent Nvidia's chips are turning into the companies that compete with it.

JalapeГұo is still in testing, with deployment slated for late this year, and it only handles inference, the running of models, so training still leans on Nvidia.В 

The 50% number comes from Broadcom. Owning the chip, the model and the product lets OpenAI squeeze cost out of every layer at once, and that's the whole game now.


----------



----------
_Special highlight from our network_

## [This Tech Could Be a Star in AIвҖҷs Future](https://link.genai.works/ZxEL)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/f36a56d6-48b2-438a-b33a-2b313d25bfcd/BluSkyAI_Newsletter_GenAIWorks_DisplayAd.png?t=1782380848)
Follow image link: (https://link.genai.works/ZxEL)
Caption: 

Goldman Sachs projects data center power demand could eclipse supply by 2030. This demand is near impossi

75054.subject: Turn Claude Into an AI Research Powerhouse With Stanford's STORM Method рҹ§
75054.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/stanford-storm-claude-ai-research

рҹ‘Ӣ Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 395k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
Almost everyone still uses Claude the same way: type вҖңresearch X,вҖқ get back the most obvious consensus framing of X.
It reads well. It is also exactly what your competitor gets when they type the same thing.
Turns out, the edge was never in the answer. It was in the questions nobody thought to ask.
StanfordвҖҷs STORM method exists to fix that problem. STORM - Synthesis of Topic Outlines through Retrieval and Multi-perspective Question Asking - was built by the Stanford OVAL Lab to research a topic from many expert perspectives, ground every claim in real sources, and write only after an outline is stable. In the Stanford teamвҖҷs evaluation, STORM articles were judged better organized by a 25-percentage-point margin over outline-driven retrieval-augmented baselines, and broader in coverage by 10 points.
The catch: the original method was designed as a Python codebase, not a workflow you can run in a chat window, so almost nobody captures its full power inside Claude.
This guide rebuilds it as Boardroom STORM: a decision-grade workflow you run inside Claude, with full attribution to the Stanford research it adapts. Instead of five generic вҖңexpert views,вҖқ Claude generates ten decision-relevant perspectives, runs source-grounded interviews for each, maintains a contradiction ledger, synthesizes a source-mapped outline, drafts only from that outline, and finishes with a Co-STORM-style moderator pass that hunts for the unknown unknowns most reports never surface.
Below, we cover the full system end-to-end:
The academic foundation explained in plain language
The six-stage operating model
All nine copy-paste prompts
Worked examples for founders, investors, & builders, and
A ready-made Claude Skill file you upload once and trigger with a single sentence
If you make decisions where being early and being right actually matters, this is the difference between AI that summarizes what everyone already knows and AI that finds what they missed.
The academic foundation - what is STORM...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.ZWRbCYissXAb1U-9mRUYSLook8llBr3x10ceG_a45nQ?
